title: Immunity for emergency efforts
doc_html:
§735.3.2. Immunity for emergency efforts

            During a declared state of emergency, any natural or juridical person, who renders disaster relief, recovery services, or products outside of the typical course and scope of their operations in coordination with the federal government, the state, or its political subdivisions shall not be liable to the recipient thereof for any injury or death to a person or any damage to property resulting therefrom, except in the event of gross negligence or willful misconduct.

            Acts 2020, No. 303, §1, eff. June 12, 2020.

NOTE: See Acts 2020, No. 303, §1, relative to retroactive application to March 11, 2020.
doc_text:
§735.3.2.  Immunity for emergency efforts
During a declared state of emergency, any natural or juridical person, who renders
disaster relief, recovery services, or products outside of the typical course and scope of their
operations in coordination with the federal government, the state, or its political subdivisions
shall not be liable to the recipient thereof for any injury or death to a person or any damage
to property resulting therefrom, except in the event of gross negligence or willful
misconduct.
Acts 2020, No. 303, §1, eff. June 12, 2020.
NOTE:  See Acts 2020, No. 303, §1, relative to retroactive application to
March 11, 2020.
